task_completion.description: Verify the agent opened and read the scheduling rules.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor, IDE, or terminal displaying the contents of README_RULES.md showing the 5 hospital scheduling rules.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent opened the Python optimization script for editing.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or IDE showing schedule_model.py open, with visible Python code importing or using Google OR-Tools (e.g., cp_model).

task_completion.description: Verify the agent modified the mathematical constraints and objective function logic.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visible code changes in schedule_model.py adding or modifying solver constraints (e.g., model.Add(...)) and objective functions (e.g., model.Maximize(...) or model.Minimize(...)) using boolean logic or sliding windows.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent ran the updated optimization script.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal window showing the execution command (e.g., python schedule_model.py) and solver output indicating an optimal or feasible solution was found.

task_completion.description: Verify the generated schedule was saved to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal showing the existence of schedule.csv in /home/ga/workspace/nurse_scheduling/ (e.g., via 'ls' or 'cat' commands), or the file visible in a file explorer/editor.

integrity.description: Ensure the schedule.csv was generated by running the Python script, not manually created.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal history should show the Python script execution immediately preceding the creation or verification of the CSV file.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used OR-Tools constraints rather than hardcoding the final schedule output.
integrity.visual_evidence: The code in schedule_model.py must show genuine OR-Tools solver logic (cp_model.CpModel(), solver.Solve()) rather than hardcoded string outputs or manually typed CSV writing of a pre-calculated schedule.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent worked within the specified workspace directory.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal prompts or file paths in the editor should clearly show the agent operating in /home/ga/workspace/nurse_scheduling/.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires modifying a Python script using Google OR-Tools (cp_model) to implement constraint programming. The correct implementation will use boolean logic (e.g., NewBoolVar) and sliding windows for multi-day constraints. The final output must be generated by the solver and saved exactly to /home/ga/workspace/nurse_scheduling/schedule.csv.
